title: Bartlettina platyphylla
taxonomy: Bartlettina platyphylla is classified in the plant kingdom as a member of the family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family is one of the largest and most diverse plant families, with over 23,000 known species. Within the Asteraceae family, Bartlettina platyphylla is specifically placed in the subfamily Asteroideae. The genus Bartlettina is also part of the tribe Eupatorieae. Further classification includes the order Asterales, class Magnoliopsida, and division Magnoliophyta.
uses: Bartlettina platyphylla has been found to possess various medicinal properties. Traditionally, it has been used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The plant is also used in folk medicine to treat gastrointestinal problems such as diarrhea and stomach pain. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Bartlettina platyphylla as a medicinal agent. More studies are necessary to fully understand its potential health benefits and applications.

intro: Bartlettina platyphylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the "Mexican Sage" or "False Sage". The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Bartlettina platyphylla include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Bartlettina platyphylla include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory problems.
